data.title: Adicet Bio announces one-for-16 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Adicet Bio (ACET) on Friday announced a one-for-16 reverse stock split of Adicet’s common stock, par value $0.0001 per share.
  • The reverse stock split will take effect on December 30, 2025, and Adicet’s common stock will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis on Nasdaq as of the opening of trading on December 30, 2025. 
  • When the reverse stock split becomes effective, every sixteen of Adicet’s issued shares of common stock will be combined into one issued share of common stock, without any change to the par value per share. This will reduce the number of outstanding shares of common stock from about 153.3 million shares to about 9.6 million shares.
  • The reverse stock split will not affect the absolute number of Adicet’s authorized shares of common stock, which will remain at 300M, but the total number of shares of Adicet’s common stock available for future issuance will increase.
  • The reverse stock split is intended to enable Adicet to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement for continued listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • ACET -19.1% premarket to $0.42.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Jeff Brands to sell 10% of Fort Technology stake
data.description:
• Shares of Jeffs’ Brands (JFBR) are gaining ground in Friday’s premarket trading after the company announced plans to trim its stake in Fort Technology amid an effort by the company to focus on homeland security and advanced technologies.
  • The sale of ~1.5M shares represents 10% of Jeff Brands’ (JFBR) stake in Fort Technology and 7.4% of total outstanding shares of Fort Technology. Following the transation, the company will continue to hold a majority stake in Fort Technology.
  • The CAD 928,571 ($679.090) proceeds of the sale will provide liquidity for Jeff Brands’ (JFBR) investments in homeland security technologies through its KeepZone AI subsidiary and Scanary distribution agreement.
  • Shares are currently up nearly 8%.

data.title: J&J eczema therapy trails expectations in mid-stage trial
data.description:
• Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) traded lower in the premarket on Friday after the New Jersey-based pharmaceutical giant announced a setback in a mid-stage trial for JNJ-95475939 (JNJ-5939), its experimental therapy for a skin condition called atopic dermatitis (eczema).
  • Citing a planned interim analysis, the company said that the results from its Phase 2b DUPLEX-AD proof-of-concept study for JNJ-95475939 in moderate to severe atopic dermatitis fell short of its expectations.
  • The results met the prespecified criteria for DUPLEX-AD’s early termination, “as they did not meet the high-bar efficacy we established for advancing our clinical development programs for atopic dermatitis,” J&J announced, adding that JNJ-5939, however, indicated a well-tolerated safety profile.
  • “We look forward to delivering new, transformative medicines that have the greatest potential to meet the needs of the more than 100 million individuals worldwide impacted by this disease.”

data.title: Precious Metals Extend Shine
data.description:
Silver on Friday scaled the $75/oz level for the first time, while gold and platinum hit new record highs, as a broader run in precious metals continued to power on at the close of the year.

Spot silver (XAGUSD:CUR) advanced as much as +1.0% to hit $75.42/oz. Spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) added as much as +1.1% to notch an all-time high of $4,530/oz, while spot platinum (XPTUSD:CUR) jumped as much as +9.0% to a peak of $2,463.60.  

Precious metals have seen outsized gains this year driven by several factors ranging from the prospects of U.S. interest rate cuts to volatility amplified by low liquidity. Silver in particular has seen a meteoric rise of +158.6% YTD on the back of supply constraints, its designation as a U.S. critical mineral, and solid industrial demand. 

"Expectations of two rate cuts next year, amidst a tolerance of above-target inflation, are also motivating purchases of (these) commodities, as the debasement trade pushes some investors into hard, less cyclical materials with limited supplies that hedge the potential for currency weakness," José Torres, senior economist at Interactive Brokers, observed this week. 

Gold breaks a 40-year correlation: According to Seeking Alpha analyst Brian Gilmartin, this year's uptrend in the yellow metal has come alongside gains in equities, breaking a negative correlation that gold had with stocks for four decades. "Is gold signaling an issue for the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq again? It’s possible, although GLD’s breakout in October 2023 has now occurred even as the S&P 500 made new highs in late 2025," he said.

Also see top silver mining stocks in terms of their Seeking Alpha Quant Rating and their YTD performance.

Here are some exchange-traded funds tied to gold, gold miners, silver, and silver miners: (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GDX), (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), (DUST), (SLV), (SIVR), (AGQ), (ZSL), (PSLV), (SIL), (SILJ), and (SLVP).

data.title: Stock futures little changed while precious metals hit new record highs
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures were little changed ahead of what is expected to be a trading session with low volumes post the Christmas holiday. Precious metals silver, gold, and platinum scaled new all-time highs, extending their massive run this year.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) rose +0.17%. S&P 500 futures (SPX) advanced +0.11%, and Dow futures (INDU) edged higher by +0.01%.

Spot silver (XAGUSD:CUR) added as much as +1.0% to hit $75.42/oz. Spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) increased as much as +1.1% to notch an all-time high of $4,530/oz, while spot platinum (XPTUSD:CUR) jumped as much as +9.0% to a peak of $2,463.60.

Traders are returning from a truncated Christmas Eve trading day on Wednesday, followed by Thursday's holiday. The year-end Santa Claus rally was off to a promising start in Wednesday's shortened session, with the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) notching a record close.

U.S. Treasury yields showed mixed movement. The longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up to 4.14%. The U.S. 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) rose to 4.80%. Meanwhile, the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) slipped to 3.50%.

Top gainers in premarket trading included Sandisk (SNDK) +3.97%, Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) +2.85%, and Albemarle (ALB) +2.35%. Decliners included Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) -1.51%, Avery Dennison (AVY) -1.04%, and Ameren (AEE) -0.84%.

data.title: CrowdStrike sees bullish views at Wedbush heading into 2026
data.description:
Wedbush said CrowdStrike (CRWD) is one of the best ways to "play the cyber meets AI theme" in 2026.

The firm maintained its Outperform rating and $600 price target on the shares of the cybersecurity provider and added that CrowdStrike remains a core winner on the IVES AI 30 (IVES) list.

"CrowdStrike remains one of our favorite tech names and we are seeing deal momentum spread with AI also a clear tailwind for this well positioned tech leader. We believe increased market and mind share is happening for CrowdStrike among new and existing customers as the company's product suite continues to expand across the enterprise landscape over the next 12 to 18 months," said analysts led by Dan Ives.

The analysts noted that the company has seen strength driven by continued platform adoption with healthy momentum in Cloud, Identity, and Logscale along with better-than expected trends in Data Protection and Charlotte AI, which saw considerable strength in deal flow in the most recent quarter and they believe it has accelerated in the January quarter thus far based on their recent field checks.

The analysts added that while AI continues to be top of mind for enterprises heading into an important year for AI monetization, they are seeing more module add-ons within CrowdStrike's core verticals as we are still in the very early innings of this trend playing out.

"Since the start of CY2025, revenue tied to cyber-AI products is significantly above the year ago period and we expect to see an accelerating upward trend but will torque everything pointing to an elevated benefit from AI cyber spending with CRWD in a strong position to continue capitalizing on the AI cybersecurity trend given the strength and stickiness of the Falcon platform," said Ives and his team.

The analysts added that due to the rising threat landscape, starting to use AI to enhance sophistication of malware attacks, CrowdStrike has taken key strategic steps to integrate AI across its portfolio to capitalize on elevated demand for advanced cyber solutions to protect IT and AI infrastructure across the tech stack.

Ives and his team noted that CrowdStrike remains the gold standard for cybersecurity with the company in the early innings of its multi-year growth story with Net New Annual Recurring Revenue, or NNARR, or growth expected to reaccelerate in 2026.

"To this point we believe the Street is underestimating the growth potential for CrowdStrike as a second/third derivative beneficiary of the AI Revolution and this speaks to our core bullishness in the name continuing from current levels. In a bull case scenario we can see CRWD shares hitting $700 over the next year as the company executes on its core growth strategy. We also continue to believe the cyber security subsector could be an outperformer within the broader tech space," said Ives and his team.

data.title: Biohaven defended despite depression trial setback
data.description:
Biohaven (BHVN) shares fell ~15% in the premarket on Friday, even as Wall Street analysts defended the biotech in reaction to its Christmas Eve announcement regarding a failure in a Phase 2 trial for its experimental depression therapy BHV-7000.

The New Haven, Connecticut-based company said on Wednesday that it will not conduct any additional psychiatric clinical trials as part of a portfolio shakeup after BHV-7000 (opakalim) missed the main goal in a Phase 2 proof-of-concept study for major depressive disorder.

According to Bloomberg, Citi analyst Samantha Semenkow, with a Buy rating on BHVN, argued that while BHV-7000’s data from the depression trial was a disappointment, investors had “very low expectations of the study working.”

William Blair’s Myles Minter agreed, noting that while the study miss is disappointing, his firm, which rates BHVN with a Market Perform recommendation, had been cautious due to “difficulties in clinical trial execution and placebo creep in trials over recent years.”

The setback comes as Biohaven (BHVN) is eyeing an H1 2026 timeline to release topline data from one of two pivotal studies evaluating 25 mg and 50 mg doses of opakalim in refractory focal epilepsy.

Citi’s Semenkow said that investors have been more focused on late-stage study data for BHV-7000 in epilepsy, expected in H1 2026. Raymond James analyst Christopher Raymond held similar views, noting that investors should “stay focused on the focal epilepsy opportunity,” and Leerink’s Marc Goodman wrote that negative trial data were “disappointing but not that surprising.”

Goodman, who has an Outperform recommendation on BHVN, identified the readout among some of the key catalysts over the next 6–9 months “that the company has been counting on to put some momentum behind the stock.”

Meanwhile, RBC Capital Markets analyst Leonid Timashev, with a Sector Perform rating, argued that the setback could be a “clearing event” heading into the 2026 catalysts that have higher probabilities of success.

data.title: China hits 20 U.S. firms with sanctions over Taiwan arms sales
data.description:
China's foreign ministry said Friday it is imposing sanctions targeting 20 U.S. defense-related companies and 10 executives, a week after the Trump administration announced more than $11 billion in arms sales to Taiwan.

The sanctioned companies include Northrop Grumman Systems Corp. (NOC), L3Harris Maritime Services (LHX), and Boeing's (BA) operations in St. Louis, Missouri.

The measures freeze any assets the companies and individuals hold in China and bar domestic organizations and individuals from doing business with them, and the individuals on the list, including the founder of Anduril Industries and nine senior executives from the sanctioned firms, are banned from entering China, the ministry said.

The actual impact of the measures likely will be minimal, since most of the companies and executives targeted have little or no presence in China, and some already were banned from entry by China's commerce ministry.

"The Taiwan question is at the very core of China's interests and the first red line that cannot be crossed in China-U.S. relations," the Chinese foreign ministry said in a statement.

data.title: Foreign phone shipments in China surge 128% in November: report
data.description:
Shipments of foreign-branded phones, including Apple's (AAPL) iPhone, in China surged 128.4% in November year-over-year, Reuters reported, citing its calculations based on data from a government-affiliated research firm.

The data from the China Academy of Information and Communications Technology, or CAICT, showed that overall phone shipments in China were up 1.9% year-over-year to 30.16 million units in November. Shipments of foreign-branded phones were 6.93 million units, the report added.

The shipments of foreign-branded phones were 7.027 million units in October 2025, showing growth of 13% year-over-year. 

China's smartphone market is dominated by companies such as vivo, Xiaomi (XIACF) (XIACY), Huawei Technologies, Apple (AAPL), Oppo, and Honor.

data.title: Leifras raises ¥2.5 billion, diversifying fundraising channels
